description: String canonicalization cases for RFC 8785. Unicode, escape sequences, and control-character handling are common divergence points across implementations.

cases.name: quote and backslash
cases.rationale: RFC 8785 requires backslash escape for quote and backslash characters

cases.name: control newline
cases.rationale: newline MUST be \n in canonical form, never raw or \u000a

cases.name: control tab
cases.rationale: tab MUST be \t in canonical form

cases.name: low control unicode
cases.rationale: control characters below 0x20 that don't have named escapes MUST use \uXXXX lowercase hex per RFC 8785

cases.name: unicode bmp
cases.rationale: BMP Unicode is passed through verbatim — RFC 8785 does NOT escape above 0x20

cases.name: unicode emoji surrogate pair
cases.rationale: supplementary plane character encoded as surrogate pair in UTF-16; canonical form preserves as single UTF-8 sequence

cases.name: forward slash
cases.rationale: RFC 8785 does NOT require escaping forward slashes (common divergence from older JSON libraries that DID escape them)
